commit.message: some fixes

perf: add thumbnail cache in UI and Tauri backend

UI (FileBrowser.tsx):
- Module-level Map (max 500 entries, LRU eviction) shared across all remounts
- FileThumbnail initialises src from cache on mount → instant display on scroll-back
- triedRef starts true when cache hit → IntersectionObserver never fires IPC

Tauri (main.rs):
- Static Mutex<HashMap> cache keyed by path, invalidated on mtime change
- Avoids re-decoding JPEG/PNG on every request from the same session

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: reduce thumbnail concurrency to 1, debounce to 200ms; add throttle tests

- THUMB_CONCURRENCY 3 → 1: sequential IPC calls prevent UI thread contention
- Debounce 80ms → 200ms: skip more aggressively during fast scroll
- Add 5 new tests covering debounce timing, scroll-out cancellation,
  no-repeat guard (triedRef), and concurrency queue behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: update Cargo.lock after adding image and base64 crates

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: throttle thumbnail IPC calls to prevent scroll jank

- Add module-level queue with max 3 concurrent get-thumbnail IPC calls
- Add 80ms debounce on IntersectionObserver to skip rapidly scrolled items
- Switch tried flag from useState to useRef to avoid re-renders

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add file thumbnails and built-in preview panel

- Inline lazy-loaded thumbnails (64px) in FileBrowser using IntersectionObserver
- FilePreviewPanel component for full-size image (512px) and text file previews
- Electron: get-thumbnail IPC handler (sharp, LRU cache) + get-file-text-preview
- Tauri: get_thumbnail command (image crate) + get_file_text_preview command
- Platform adapter (tauri.ts) wired up for both new channels
- useBuiltinViewer setting: toggle between built-in viewer and OS default app
- Settings UI: "File Viewer" toggle in Tools tab with ON/OFF descriptions
- i18n keys added to en.json and ru.json
- Tests: 55 passing across file-browser, file-preview-panel, settings-modal-viewer

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use model ID instead of display name in fallback model resolution (#110)

When a session model is resolved via the fallback path (no :: prefix),
the code was using matchingModel.name which contains the display name
(e.g. "Qwen: Qwen3 235B A22B Instruct 2507 (qwen/qwen3-235b-a22b-2507)")
instead of extracting the actual model ID from matchingModel.id.

This caused OpenRouter API to reject requests with "is not a valid model ID".

Fix: extract model ID from the composite id field (providerId::modelId)
instead of using the display name.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

tree efb50e273cdcd6cf5e6856159e8c3029ed21a700
parent d324abc271d0ca8a8c2e7fbe626046e2c3e84ee0
author Aleksandr Poliakov <42345388+artwist-polyakov@users.noreply.github.com> 1771450763 +0300
committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1771450763 +0300

fix: use model ID instead of display name in fallback model resolution (#110)

When a session model is resolved via the fallback path (no :: prefix),
the code was using matchingModel.name which contains the display name
(e.g. "Qwen: Qwen3 235B A22B Instruct 2507 (qwen/qwen3-235b-a22b-2507)")
instead of extracting the actual model ID from matchingModel.id.

This caused OpenRouter API to reject requests with "is not a valid model ID".

Fix: extract model ID from the composite id field (providerId::modelId)
instead of using the display name.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: correct token counting on frontend — use additive accumulation

Frontend was replacing token counts with per-run values instead of
adding them. When user sends a second message in the same session:
- Runner starts with totalInputTokens=0, accumulates only current run
- Frontend was setting inputTokens = input_tokens (REPLACE)
- Should be inputTokens += input_tokens (ADD)

Now frontend correctly adds per-run token counts to session totals,
matching the additive behavior of updateTokens() in the backend DB.

fix: prevent agent from reading skill scripts — execute only

- Removed 'read_file' operation from load_skill tool entirely
- Agent can only: get SKILL.md, list_files, list_available
- read_file tool now blocks reading skills/*/scripts/* files
- Updated all prompts: 'execute scripts via bash, do not read source'
- Prevents agent from dumping 700-line Python scripts into chat

The agent should run skill scripts (python scripts/foo.py), not read them.

security: block reading .env files to prevent secret leakage

- read_file tool now returns error when trying to read .env files
- grep/search_text tool excludes .env files from search results
- System prompt explicitly forbids reading .env files
- Skills prompt updated: removed .env mention, added 'ask user for env vars'
- Prevents agent from accidentally exposing API keys, tokens, passwords

The JSONDecodeError was caused by agent reading .env (not JSON) and
trying to json.loads() it. Blocking .env access prevents this class
of errors entirely.

fix: include skill directory path in skill tool output and system prompt

- getSkill() now includes the full skill directory path in output
- listSkillFiles() shows skill directory path for reference
- readSkillFile() shows full file path
- System prompt skills section now includes instructions to resolve
  relative paths (scripts/, config/, .env) relative to the skill
  directory, NOT the user's working directory
- Follows Codex CLI pattern where skill path is always visible to agent

Fixes issue where agent looked for .env in project root instead of
in the skill's own config/ directory

fix: table rendering — remove whitespace-nowrap from table, allow text wrapping in cells

- Removed whitespace-nowrap from <table> element (was preventing text wrapping in all cells)
- Added w-full so table fills container width evenly
- Moved whitespace-nowrap to <th> only (headers should stay on one line)
- Replaced inline style maxWidth with Tailwind max-w-full
- Fixes issue where long cell content would overflow or tables looked broken

fix: configurable API timeout (#107), reject binary files in write_file (#80)

- Add requestTimeoutMs setting (default 300s) with UI in Settings > Memory & Mode
  Allows users with slow models (LM Studio) to increase timeout (#107)
- Reject binary/structured formats (.docx, .pdf, .xlsx, etc.) in write_file tool
  with helpful error pointing to execute_python or load_skill (#80)
- Use model.name instead of model.id in fallback model resolution

fix: use model.name instead of model.id when resolving models without provider prefix

model.id contains the full providerId::modelName format which gets sent
to the API server as-is, causing 404 errors. Now uses model.name which
contains just the model name (e.g. 'gpt-oss-120b' instead of
'openai-local-1771356257729::gpt-oss-120b').

feat: conversation compact + model resolution fallback (#108)

* feat: add conversation compact/summarize feature + fix model resolution fallback

* fix: handle session.compact event in sidecar + add compact types to ui/types.ts

* fix: enrich session.compact with DB session data so sidecar can restore it

* fix: intercept session.list from sidecar and replace with full DB list

* fix: pass LLM provider settings from Rust DB to sidecar compact + add restoreSession to avoid sync:create conflict

* fix: fallback to first available provider when session's provider is missing

tree a3be1909997a4b92cb34895fecdecdeb5dd3de28
parent b69dda25f5ae102363aad27818d5ddb57c548bb9
author Valerii Kovalskii <kovalskyvr@mail.ru> 1771358340 +0300
committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1771358340 +0300

commit.message:
fix: cross-platform sidecar build, token display, and several bug fixes (#105)

- Make build:sidecar cross-platform by detecting OS/arch at runtime
  instead of hardcoding macOS arm64 target (scripts/build-sidecar.cjs)
- Fix token count display for OpenAI-compatible servers (llama.cpp, vLLM)
  by supporting both prompt_tokens/completion_tokens and
  input_tokens/output_tokens field names
- Fix OpenRouter model fetch crash when pricing data is missing
- Fix unused sessionId variable that broke production builds (tsc strict)
- Improve error message when legacy API settings are not configured
  to hint about LLM Provider model selection

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: update model handling and default settings across components

- Updated model retrieval logic in various components to prioritize scheduler default model and API settings.
- Enhanced error handling for missing model configurations in session management.
- Improved debug logging for OpenAI runner to assist in troubleshooting.
- Removed deprecated YAML dependency from package-lock.json.
- Incremented versions of 'tar' and 'vm2' packages in package-lock.json for security and stability.

feat: enhance role group task handling and UI integration

- Implemented detailed handling for 'role_group' mode in task creation and session management.
- Updated UI components to support role group task creation, including validation for prompts.
- Enhanced RoleGroupDialog to build prompts based on selected roles and task details.
- Introduced new types for role group settings in the application.
- Improved session broadcasting and error handling for role group tasks.

feat: introduce role group task management and UI components

- Added support for 'role_group' task mode in task handling logic.
- Implemented RoleGroupDialog for creating tasks with specific roles.
- Updated UI components to display role group tasks and their details.
- Enhanced settings modal to manage role group configurations.
- Introduced new types for role group settings and roles in the application.
- Updated sidebar and task item components to accommodate role group functionality.

commit.message:
feat(tests): add Vitest configuration and initial test cases

- Introduced Vitest configuration file for testing setup.
- Added test cases for duckduckgo search tool, memory tool, runner-openai tool, session store, skills loader, and skills tool.
- Updated package.json to include Vitest as a dependency and added a test script.
- Enhanced README with testing instructions.

fix(memory): use global user-level memory instead of workspace-local (#90)

Memory is now always stored in ~/.localdesk/memory.md (global)
instead of project-local {cwd}/.localdesk/memory.md

This makes memory persistent across all projects and sessions.

commit.message:
Release v0.0.8 (#86)

* tauri initial and working

* macos bundle build

* robust check tools via make

* make fix

* fix: add Rust build artifacts to gitignore, auto-rebuild better-sqlite3

* feat: dynamic tools summary, fix naming, improve DX

- Tool naming: Scheduler → schedule_task, download → download_file
- System prompt: dynamic tools summary instead of hardcoded list
- generateToolsSummary() groups tools by category
- Makefile: add Rust version check (min 1.74.0)
- Makefile: add @yao-pkg/pkg to devDependencies for bundle
- Rust: suppress dead_code warnings during migration
- Rust: fix duplicate node_bin variable
- CSS: fix select dropdown styling for Tauri WebView
- Update .cursor/rules documentation

* dev build FIxes for win 10 +

* fixes for win 10 - make bundle

* v0.0.7: session persistence, Python sandbox, improved architecture

Major changes:
- Session persistence: sessions survive app restart (SQLite in Rust)
- Stop button: properly interrupts agent with AbortController
- Reset stale running sessions on startup
- Python sandbox: system Python with pip package support
- JS sandbox: Node.js vm module
- Skills download to {cwd}/skills/ instead of .localdesk
- Reduced log noise (skip non-JSON debug lines)

Code reorganization:
- Renamed src/electron/ to src/agent/
- Added db.rs for SQLite operations
- Added sandbox.rs (unused, logic in sidecar)
- Updated documentation (README, CURSOR.md, .cursor/rules/)

Fixes:
- Fixed dynamic import issue in quickjs-sandbox.ts
- Improved load_skill output to prevent model confusion
- Session status properly updates on stop

* docs: update README with Tauri build instructions

* chore: remove CHANGELOG.md

* fix: make createdAt/updatedAt optional in LLMProvider, fix provider deletion

* fix: show provider name instead of type in model selectors

* chore: bump version to 0.0.8

* chore: update version to 0.0.8 and add build-info and linux-schema files

---------

Co-authored-by: Gleb Kudriavtcev <glebkudr@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: hijera <hi-jera@yandex.ru>
Co-authored-by: snow-ghost <spec256@ya.ru>

- Fix IPC validateEventFrame() to handle URL-encoded Cyrillic paths
- Fix FileBrowser list-directory path comparison
- Fix tools-executor isPathSafe() for Cyrillic paths
- Fix dev script batch file termination prompt on Windows

Changes:
- Use decodeURIComponent() for URL path comparison
- Use toLowerCase() for case-insensitive comparison (Windows)
- Use normalize('NFC') for Unicode normalization
- Use async spawn with ignored stdin in dev script

Fixes #70

commit.message:
feat: Skills System Integration (#61)

Add Agent Skills integration from LocalDesk-Skills marketplace.

## Features

### Skills Store (Backend)
- skills-store.ts: Persistence for enabled skills
- skills-loader.ts: Fetch skills from GitHub repo
- skills-tool.ts: Tool for agent to load/use skills

### Skills UI (Frontend)
- SkillsTab.tsx: Skills management in Settings modal
- Search/filter by name and category
- Enable/disable individual skills
- Custom marketplace URL support

### System Integration
- IPC handlers for skills events
- System prompt includes enabled skills
- Dynamic skills section generation

### How It Works
1. User enables skills in Settings > Skills
2. Enabled skills are mentioned in system prompt
3. Agent can use load_skill tool to get detailed instructions
4. Agent follows SKILL.md guidance for specialized tasks

Closes #61

releases.name: LocalDesk v0.0.8
releases.body:
## Changes in v0.0.8

### Bug Fixes
- Fixed LLM provider settings not saving correctly
- Show provider name instead of type in model selectors
- Fixed sessions remaining in 'running' status after app restart
- Fixed agent stop button not interrupting ongoing requests
- Reduced noisy debug logs from sidecar

### Improvements
- Better Python sandbox support with pip packages
- Improved skill download paths
- Updated system prompts for better code execution guidance

releases.name: LocalDesk v0.0.7
releases.body:
## What's New in LocalDesk 0.0.7

### Skills System Integration
- **Skills Marketplace** - Browse and enable skills from [LocalDesk-Skills](https://github.com/vakovalskii/LocalDesk-Skills) repository
- **load_skill Tool** - Agent can now load detailed instructions and scripts for specialized tasks
- **Skills Tab** in Settings - Search, filter, and manage enabled skills

### Image Support
- **attach_image Tool** - Attach local images (auto-converted to WebP) for model input

### Bug Fixes
- Fixed JSON parsing errors when models return malformed tool arguments
- Improved error handling in tool execution

### Downloads
- **LocalDesk 0.0.7.exe** - Windows portable version (no installation required)
- **LocalDesk 0.0.7.msi** - Windows installer
- **LocalDesk 0.0.7.AppImage** - Linux portable version (no installation required)

releases.name: LocalDesk v0.0.5
releases.body:
## What's Changed

### 🐛 Bug Fixes
- **Fix session title generation** - use OpenAI API instead of Claude SDK
- **Fix 'require is not defined'** in ExecuteJS sandbox (ES modules compatibility)

### ✨ New Features
- **Dynamic sandbox packages loading** - installed npm packages now shown in system prompt
- **Improved system prompt** - detailed ExecuteJS examples and instructions

### 📦 Project Updates
- Rename project: Agent Cowork → LocalDesk
- Updated all paths: `.agent-cowork` → `.localdesk`
- Updated README with macOS/Linux and Windows instructions

### 📥 Download
- **macOS ARM64 (Apple Silicon)**: `LocalDesk-0.0.5-arm64.dmg`

releases.name: v0.0.4 - JS Sandbox & PDF Support
releases.body:
## 🚀 New Features
- ✅ **JavaScript Sandbox** - Execute JS code in isolated Node.js VM
- ✅ **InstallPackage Tool** - Install npm packages into `.cowork-sandbox/`
- ✅ **PDF Support** - Extract text from PDFs using `pdf-parse`
- ✅ **Optional Workspace** - Start empty chats, add workspace later
- ✅ **Stop Button** - Interrupt LLM streaming at any time

## 📦 Downloads
- **Windows MSI** - Full installer (156 MB)
- **Windows Portable** - Single EXE, no installation (210 MB)
- **macOS DMG** - Coming soon!

## 🔧 Improvements
- Fixed Cyrillic filenames support on Windows
- Improved glob tool with Node.js fs API
- Enhanced security with proper path sandboxing

releases.name: v0.0.3 - Memory System & UI Improvements
releases.body:
## 🎉 What's New

### Major Features
- 🧠 **Memory System** - Persistent storage for user preferences in `~/.agent-cowork/memory.md`
- 🔄 **Dynamic Memory Reload** - Memory updates are immediately available within the same session
- ✏️ **Message Editing** - Edit and resend messages with automatic history truncation
- 📌 **Session Pinning** - Pin important sessions and search through chat history
- 📜 **Improved Auto-scroll** - Better handling of long streaming messages

### UI/UX Improvements
- ⌨️ **Keyboard Shortcuts** - Cmd+Enter (Mac) / Ctrl+Enter (Windows) to send messages
- 🔍 **Spell Check** - Built-in spell checking with context menu suggestions
- 📁 **Open in Finder** - Button to open current directory in file manager
- 💎 **Visual Highlights** - Pinned sessions are visually highlighted in sidebar
- 📊 **Token Tracking** - Display input/output tokens and API duration

### System Improvements
- ✅ **Fixed Permission System** - Ask/default modes now work correctly
- 📦 **Compressed Tool History** - Efficient tool execution history format
- 🌍 **Language Consistency** - Agent responds in the same language as user
- 🛡️ **Write Tool Protection** - Prevents accidental file overwriting
- 📝 **Raw JSON Logging** - Full request/response logs for debugging
- 🗑️ **Removed AskUserQuestion** - Simplified tool set (redundant tool removed)

### Memory System Usage
```
User: "Remember that I prefer Python over JavaScript"
Agent: [Stores in memory.md]

User: "What language do I prefer?"
Agent: "You prefer Python over JavaScript" ✅
```

### Installation
Download `Agent Cowork-0.0.3-arm64.dmg` for macOS (Apple Silicon)

### Files
- `Agent Cowork-0.0.3-arm64.dmg` - Main installer (160 MB)
- `Agent Cowork-0.0.3-arm64.dmg.blockmap` - For auto-updates
- `latest-mac.yml` - Update metadata

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/vakovalskii/Cowork-Local-LLM/compare/v0.0.2...v0.0.3

readme_html:
ValeDesk

Versatile Almost Local, Eventually Reasonable Assistant

Desktop AI Assistant with Local Model Support

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a8c54ce0-2fe0-40c3-8018-026cab9d7483

✨ Features

Core Capabilities

  • ✅ Task Planning — visual todo panel with progress tracking, persisted per session
  • ✅ Scheduled Tasks — create reminders and recurring tasks with auto-execution
  • ✅ OpenAI SDK — full API control, compatible with any OpenAI-compatible endpoint
  • ✅ Local Models — vLLM, Ollama, LM Studio support
  • ✅ Code Sandboxes — JavaScript (Node.js vm) and Python (system subprocess) execution
  • ✅ Document Support — PDF and DOCX text extraction (bundled, works out of the box)
  • ✅ Web Search — Tavily and Z.AI integration for internet search
  • ✅ Telegram Parsing — render t.me channels with reactions, views, auto-scroll for older posts
  • ✅ Security — directory sandboxing for safe file operations
  • ✅ Cross-platform — Windows, macOS, Linux with proper shell commands

UI/UX Features

  • ✅ Modern Interface — React + Tauri with smooth auto-scroll and streaming
  • ✅ Message Editing — edit and resend messages with history truncation
  • ✅ Session Persistence — sessions survive app restart (SQLite backed)
  • ✅ Session Management — pin important sessions, search through chat history
  • ✅ Keyboard Shortcuts — Cmd+Enter/Ctrl+Enter to send messages
  • ✅ Spell Check — built-in spell checking with context menu suggestions
  • ✅ Permission System — ask/default modes for tool execution control

Advanced Features

  • ✅ Memory System — persistent storage of user preferences in ~/.valera/memory.md
  • ✅ Token Tracking — display input/output tokens and API duration
  • ✅ Optimized Streaming — requestAnimationFrame-based UI updates (60fps)
  • ✅ Stop Streaming — interrupt LLM responses at any time
  • ✅ Loop Detection — automatic detection of stuck tool call loops (5+ sequential same-tool calls)
  • ✅ Request Timeouts — 5-minute timeout with auto-retry for LLM requests
  • ✅ Session Logging — full request/response JSON logs per iteration in ~/.valera/logs/sessions/

🤔 Why ValeDesk?

Open Architecture & Full Control

ValeDesk isn't just another AI assistant — it's a framework you own. Built with TypeScript and Tauri, every component is transparent and modifiable:

  • Readable codebase — well-structured, documented code you can understand
  • Easy customization — add new tools, modify prompts, change UI without black boxes
  • Your rules — adjust behavior, safety limits, and workflows to match your needs
  • No vendor lock-in — works with any OpenAI-compatible API (vLLM, Ollama, LM Studio)

100% Local & Private

Everything runs on your machine:

  • Local inference — use Ollama, vLLM, or LM Studio for complete privacy
  • No data collection — your conversations never leave your computer
  • Offline capable — works without internet (except web search tools)
  • Sandboxed execution — secure JavaScript sandbox and file operation restrictions

Experiment & Iterate

Perfect for developers, researchers, and AI enthusiasts:

  • Test local models — compare Qwen, Llama, DeepSeek, and others
  • Debug API calls — full request/response logs for every interaction
  • Prototype tools — add custom functions in minutes
  • Monitor performance — track tokens, timing, and resource usage

Real Use Cases

# Run Ollama locally (free, 100% private)
ollama serve
# Configure ValeDesk: http://localhost:11434/v1

# Or use vLLM for faster inference
vllm serve Qwen/Qwen2.5-14B-Instruct --port 8000
# Configure ValeDesk: http://localhost:8000/v1

TL;DR: ValeDesk gives you the power of ChatGPT/Claude with the freedom of open source and privacy of local execution.

🚀 Quick Start

Prerequisites

  • Rust 1.74+ (install)
  • Node.js 20+
  • Python 3 (for execute_python tool)

Development (macOS/Linux)

# Clone and enter
git clone https://github.com/vakovalskii/ValeDesk.git
cd ValeDesk

# Install dependencies
npm install

# Run in development mode
make dev

Tests

npm run test

Build Standalone App

# Build DMG (macOS)
make bundle

# Output: ValeDesk-0.0.8.dmg

Manual Build Steps

# 1. Build sidecar binary
npm run build:sidecar

# 2. Build Tauri app
cd src-tauri && cargo build --release

# 3. Create DMG
hdiutil create -volname "ValeDesk" \
  -srcfolder src-tauri/target/release/bundle/macos/ValeDesk.app \
  -ov -format UDZO ValeDesk-0.0.8.dmg

Windows (coming soon)

Windows build requires cross-compilation setup. Check .github/workflows/ for CI builds.

Configuration

  1. Click Settings (⚙️) in the app
  2. Configure your API:
    • API Key — your key (or dummy-key for local models)
    • Base URL — API endpoint (must include /v1)
    • Model Name — model identifier
    • Temperature — 0.0-2.0 (default: 0.3)
  3. Click Save Settings

Example Configurations

Local vLLM:

{
  "apiKey": "dummy-key",
  "baseUrl": "http://localhost:8000/v1",
  "model": "qwen3-30b-a3b-instruct-2507"
}

OpenAI:

{
  "apiKey": "sk-...",
  "baseUrl": "https://api.openai.com/v1",
  "model": "gpt-4"
}

🎯 Skills Marketplace

Browse and install verified skills for ValeDesk: Skills Marketplace

🛠️ Available Tools

All tools follow snake_case naming convention (verb_noun pattern):

File Operations

Tool Description
run_command Execute shell commands (PowerShell/bash)
read_file Read text file contents
write_file Create new files
edit_file Modify files (search & replace)
search_files Find files by glob pattern (*.pdf, src/**/*.ts)
search_text Search text content in files (grep)
read_document Extract text from PDF/DOCX (max 10MB)

Code Execution

Tool Description
execute_js Run JavaScript in secure Node.js vm sandbox
execute_python Run Python code (system Python with pip packages)

Web Tools

Tool Description
search_web Search the internet (Tavily/Z.AI)
extract_page Extract full page content (Tavily only)
read_page Read web page content (Z.AI Reader)
render_page Render JS-heavy pages via Chromium (Telegram, SPAs)

Task Management

Tool Description
manage_todos Create/update task plans with visual progress tracking

Scheduler

Tool Description
schedule_task Create, list, update, delete scheduled tasks

Features:

  • One-time reminders — "remind me in 30 minutes"
  • Recurring tasks — every minute, hour, day, week, month
  • Auto-execution — tasks with prompts automatically start new chat sessions
  • Native notifications — macOS system notifications
  • Default model — set preferred model for scheduled tasks

Memory

Tool Description
manage_memory Store/read persistent user preferences

Security: All file operations are sandboxed to the workspace folder only.

📦 Building

Windows

# Build executable and installer
npm run dist:win

# Output: dist/ValeDesk Setup 0.0.8.exe

macOS

# Build DMG (ARM64)
npm run dist:mac-arm64

# Build DMG (Intel x64)
npm run dist:mac-x64

Linux

# Build AppImage
npm run dist:linux

🔐 Data Storage

Application Data

  • Windows: C:\Users\YourName\AppData\Roaming\ValeDesk\
  • macOS: ~/Library/Application Support/ValeDesk/
  • Linux: ~/.config/ValeDesk/

Files:

  • sessions.db — SQLite database with chat history, todos, scheduled tasks, and settings
  • api-settings.json — API configuration
  • skills-settings.json — Skills marketplace configuration

Global Data

  • ~/.valera/memory.md — persistent memory storage
  • ~/.valera/logs/sessions/{session-id}/ — per-session API logs:
    • turn-001-request.json — full request (model, messages, tools, temperature)
    • turn-001-response.json — full response (usage, content, tool_calls)
